text.tspan: 01 · THE WHOLE JOURNEY IS A REAL GRAPH
Default state: stable left-to-right tree, active route emphasized, every open question attached to its parent.
Why do cities preserve some memories and erase others?
Journey graph · You are at turn 8
Search turns, topics, or open paths
WHY THIS FEELS LIKE A TREE, NOT A HAIRBALL
One direction, orthogonal edges, one node type for researched turns, and a smaller leaf type for open questions.
02 · SELECT A TURN WITHOUT LOSING THE TREE
Focus state: keep node positions fixed, highlight ancestry + immediate choices, open a non-destructive inspector.
Why do cities preserve some memories and erase others?
Root › archaeology › civic power › selected turn 4
Search turns, topics, or open paths
The question is who may define place.
A · Who gets to rename a public place?
Selection changes emphasis and details, not coordinates. Closing the inspector returns to the exact same spatial position.
Dense state: semantic zoom, collapsed branch clusters, pinned active route, and minimap orientation.
Why do cities preserve some memories and erase others?
Focus mode · active route + branch summaries
Search turns, topics, or open paths
Zoom level 42% · researched turns become compact markers; meaningful branch groups keep names and counts.
At low zoom: shape, position, path, and counts. At medium zoom: titles. At high zoom: complete turn cards and actions.
04 · FIND A TURN OR OPEN PATH IN SECONDS
Search state: matches stay bright, the route to each match highlights, and off-screen results get directional markers.
Why do cities preserve some memories and erase others?
Journey graph · You are at turn 8
1 Why do cities remove controversial statues?
2 Can a removed statue still shape memory?
3 Who decides what replaces a statue?
SEARCH DOES NOT TURN THE GRAPH INTO A LIST
The result list supports scanning; the highlighted route answers “where is this?” and “how did I get there?” at the same time.
05 · PREVIEW A BRANCH BEFORE RESEARCH STARTS
Decision state: the old branch remains visible, a ghost node shows the structural result, and the cost/action is explicit.
Why do cities preserve some memories and erase others?
Current turn 8 · previewing branch from turn 3
Search turns, topics, or open paths
WonderDrive will research one new turn from Turn 3.
The result appears as a sibling branch and becomes current.
Before committing, the user sees the parent, the new location, what stays untouched, and the cost of the action.
06 · MOBILE IS GRAPH-FIRST, NOT A SHRUNK DESKTOP
Mobile state: focused neighborhood, swipe/pan canvas, bottom-sheet details, and a first-class outline alternative.
Same journey, readable without spatial navigation
Who gets to rename a place?
Keyboard: arrows move · Enter selects · Left/Right collapse or expand
The canvas is the visual experience; Outline is not a fallback afterthought—it exposes the same hierarchy, selection, and actions.
